articles.title: WHAT WE FIGHT FOR
articles.summary: Product leadership means knowing what we fight for: our users, our teams, and the long-term health of the business. Great product leaders bring heart and clear-eyed judgment to this complex role - championing users, empowering teams, and shaping company decisions with integrity and business acumen.

articles.title: THE RISE OF THE AI-NATIVE EMPLOYEE
articles.summary: Lovable prioritizes AI-driven autonomy over traditional processes. This shift has boosted productivity, removed organizational bloat, and accelerated innovation.

articles.title: AGENCY VS AMBITION
articles.summary: Not all product people have the ambition and agency needed to build empowered teams, yet those qualities are essential for progress.

articles.title: THE 250K PER EMPLOYEE CULTURAL KPI CAUSING LAYOFFS IN TECH
articles.summary: As SaaS competition grows, product managers must prioritize commercial impact over vanity projects to protect their roles. By clearly linking their work to revenue, limiting unprofitable investments, and showcasing tangible results, PMs make themselves indispensable.

articles.title: YOU SHOULD BE PLAYING WITH AI AGENTS FOR MARKETING
articles.summary: Hiring and managing AI agents is now a must-have skill. Companies like Relay.app show what's possible. CEO Jacob Bank replaced a five-person marketing team with over 40 AI agents, proving the future belongs to those who delegate effectively to AI.

articles.title: THE HIDDEN TAX OF BEING THE 'RELIABLE' PRODUCT MANAGER
articles.summary: Reliable product managers risk burnout by habitually overextending themselves, causing disrupted priorities and ineffective delegation. To maintain balance, they should clarify role boundaries, pause before reacting, and build support systems with colleagues.

articles.title: CORPOSPEAK: WHY YOU STILL SOUND LIKE A FACELESS CORPORATE ENTITY
articles.summary: Traditional "corpospeak" marketing fails because consumers now seek genuine interactions rather than polished, impersonal messaging. Successful brands must empower founders and product teams to communicate directly and authentically with customers.

articles.title: THE PROBLEM WITH VALUE HIERARCHIES (14 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: Traditional hierarchical models oversimplify strategy by suggesting top-down execution, missing upward insights, dynamic feedback loops, and varied time-to-impact. Effective organizations use flexible, interconnected artifacts and rituals to better represent complex realities across strategy, outcomes, execution, and culture.

articles.title: THE ANATOMY OF A NOTIFICATION
articles.summary: Notifications aren't just messages - they're part of a full experience with four key stages: Trigger, Hook, Destination, and Action.

articles.title: THE NEW PRODUCT TRIO
articles.summary: AI-driven growth demands expanding traditional product teams (PM/Eng/Design) to include Product Marketing and Growth, ensuring products succeed amid rising competition.
